### Jupyter notebook / Jupyter Lab version

_No response_

### Expected behavior

Running code below in Bokeh 2.4.3 will result in two renderers as shown in the screenshot below


### Observed behavior

Unless you comment out one of the _map_fig.add_tile(..)_ calls no figure will be drawn in the browser and the console wil log: _Error rendering Bokeh items: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading ‘rendering_target’)_


### Example code

```Python
from bokeh.plotting import figure
import bokeh.models as bokeh_models
from bokeh.io import curdoc
from bokeh.models import Range1d

# define figure
bounds = [
    210000,
    544000,
    250000,
    625000
  ]
x_range = Range1d(start=bounds[0], end=bounds[2], min_interval=100)
y_range = Range1d(start=bounds[1], end=bounds[3], min_interval=100)
map_fig = figure(x_range=x_range,
        y_range=y_range,)

map_fig.xgrid.grid_line_color = None
map_fig.ygrid.grid_line_color = None

# add background
tile_source = bokeh_models.BBoxTileSource(
    url=(
            "https://service.pdok.nl/hwh/luchtfotorgb/wms/v1_0?"
            "service=WMS&version=1.3.0&request=GetMap&layers=Actueel_orthoHR"
            "&width=265&height=265&styles=&crs=EPSG:28992&format=image/jpeg"
            "&bbox={XMIN},{YMIN},{XMAX},{YMAX}"
        )
        )
map_fig.add_tile(tile_source, name="background")

# add second tile overlay
second_tile_source = bokeh_models.BBoxTileSource(
    url=("https://arcgis.noorderzijlvest.nl/server/rest/services/Watergangen/"
         "Watergangen/MapServer/export?dpi=96&bbox={XMIN},{YMIN},{XMAX},{YMAX}"
         "&bboxSR=28992&transparent=true&f=image&format=png8"
)
)

map_fig.add_tile(second_tile_source, name="water")


curdoc().add_root(map_fig)
```

we only have a few common CSS variables defined in `bokehjs/src/less/base.less`. There are also private variables spread across many components. The goal is to extract common variables related to colors, backgrounds, icons, animations, etc. and then apply them in all components. This work will have to be done in stages, because it's currently unclear what will be the level of detail or specificity of these variables. Also at this point we want to capture commonality between components, without affecting their functionality and styling, as much as possible. Previously we planned to accomplish everything related to CSS-based theming in Bokeh 3.7, but that seems unrealistic. Thus any breaking changes that would affect Panel, etc., will have to be done for Bokeh 3.8.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/14177/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/14177/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

+import {expect} from "assertions"
+import type {PlotActions} from "../../../interactive"
+import {actions, xy} from "../../../interactive"
+import {display} from "../../_util"
+
+import type {PlotView} from "@bokehjs/models/plots/plot"
+import {Plot, Range1d, LinearAxis, BoxAnnotation} from "@bokehjs/models"
+import {no_repeated} from "@bokehjs/core/util/iterator"
+
+describe("BoxAnnotation", () => {
+
+  async function mkplot(box: BoxAnnotation): Promise<PlotView> {
+    const plot = new Plot({
+      width: 400,
+      height: 400,
+      min_border: 0,
+      x_range: new Range1d({start: 0, end: 1}),
+      y_range: new Range1d({start: 0, end: 1}),
+    })
+    plot.add_layout(box)
+    plot.add_layout(new LinearAxis(), "above")
+    plot.add_layout(new LinearAxis(), "left")
+    const {view} = await display(plot)
+    return view
+  }
+
+  function get_cursor(plot_view: PlotView): string {
+    return getComputedStyle(plot_view.canvas_view.events_el).cursor
+  }
+
+  async function get_cursors(ac: PlotActions, events: Iterable<UIEvent>): Promise<string[]> {
+    const cursors = []
+    for await (const _ of ac._emit(events)) {
+      cursors.push(get_cursor(ac.target))
+    }
+    return cursors
+  }
+
+  describe("when movable without handles", () => {
+    function mkbox() {
+      return new BoxAnnotation({
+        left: 0,
+        top: 0,
+        right: 0.5,
+        bottom: 0.5,
+        editable: true,
+        movable: "both",
+      })
+    }
+
+    it("should set cursor to \"grab\" inside box", async () => {
+      const plot_view = await mkplot(mkbox())
+      const ac = actions(plot_view, {units: "data"})
+
+      // inside box
+      await ac.hover(xy(0.25, 0.25))
+      expect(get_cursor(plot_view)).to.be.equal("grab")
+
+      // outside box
+      await ac.hover(xy(0.75, 0.75))
+      expect(get_cursor(plot_view)).to.be.equal("default")
+    })
+
+    it("should set cursor to \"grabbing\" when panning", async () => {
+      const plot_view = await mkplot(mkbox())
+      const ac = actions(plot_view, {units: "data"})
+
+      // First, hover to put the cursor in the correct initial state ("grab")
+      await ac.hover(xy(0.25, 0.25))
+
+      // Next, pan
+      const events = ac._pan({type: "line", xy0: xy(0.25, 0.25), xy1: xy(0.75, 0.75), n: 5})
+      const cursors = await get_cursors(ac, events)
+
+      expect([...no_repeated(cursors)]).to.be
+        .equal([...no_repeated(["grab", "grabbing", "grab"])])
+    })
+  })
+
+  describe("when movable with handles", () => {
+    function mkbox() {
+      return new BoxAnnotation({
+        left: 0,
+        top: 0,
+        right: 0.5,
+        bottom: 0.5,
+        editable: true,
+        movable: "both",
+        use_handles: true,
+      })
+    }
+
+    it("should set cursor to \"move\" in the middle", async () => {
+      const plot_view = await mkplot(mkbox())
+      const ac = actions(plot_view, {units: "data"})
+
+      // inside box
+      await ac.hover(xy(0.25, 0.25))
+      expect(get_cursor(plot_view)).to.be.equal("move")
+
+      // outside box
+      await ac.hover(xy(0.75, 0.75))
+      expect(get_cursor(plot_view)).to.be.equal("default")
+    })
+
+    it("should set cursor to \"move\" when panning", async () => {
+      const plot_view = await mkplot(mkbox())
+      const ac = actions(plot_view, {units: "data"})
+
+      // First, hover to put the cursor in the correct initial state ("move")
+      await ac.hover(xy(0.25, 0.25))
+
+      // Next, pan
+      const events = ac._pan({type: "line", xy0: xy(0.25, 0.25), xy1: xy(0.75, 0.75), n: 5})
+      const cursors = await get_cursors(ac, events)
+
+      expect(cursors).to.be
+        .equal(["move", "move", "default", "move", "move", "move", "move"])","path":"bokehjs/test/unit/models/annotations/box_annotation.ts","commit_id":"175ec51264456695556c64808de814f70d53b50e","original_commit_id":"35e12f4dc5a46887cd8272c4c27e3a258c604be1","user":{"login":"mattpap","id":27475,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI3NDc1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/27475?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap","html_url":"https://github.com/mattpap","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"I'm pretty sure this isn't going to be a reliable test as-is, because quite likely `"default"` value will appear in various places in this sequence. ### Changes, screenshots

Default cursor for moving the box selection is now the hand "grab" cursor instead of the "move" cursor.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3f7d874a-65c5-487b-8298-043285498a8c)

When dragging the box selection, the cursor changes from "grab" to "grabbing".

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f40ae4b-4e2b-478e-8bbf-68b18a920254)

Box handle cursors are unchanged.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2885294b-bfe9-4d66-8dd4-a66af43f9e9c)

However, box handle cursors now persist while the handle is being dragged.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e52c1d7f-98a2-4b26-83eb-ced805c18e83)

[x] issues: fixes #13027
- [ ] tests added / passed
- [ ] release document entry (if new feature or API change)

### Changes, screenshots

Default cursor for moving the box selection is now the hand "grab" cursor instead of the "move" cursor.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3f7d874a-65c5-487b-8298-043285498a8c)

When dragging the box selection, the cursor changes from "grab" to "grabbing".

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f40ae4b-4e2b-478e-8bbf-68b18a920254)

Box handle cursors are unchanged.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2885294b-bfe9-4d66-8dd4-a66af43f9e9c)

However, box handle cursors now persist while the handle is being dragged.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e52c1d7f-98a2-4b26-83eb-ced805c18e83)

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/pulls/comments/1871194070","pull_request_review_id":2481378719,"id":1871194070,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOADqB3M5viCfW","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,60 @@
+import numpy as np
+
+from bokeh.layouts import gridplot
+from bokeh.models import CustomAction, CustomJS
+from bokeh.plotting import figure, show
+
+x = np.linspace(0, 4*np.pi, 100)
+y = np.sin(x)
+
+TOOLS = "pan,wheel_zoom,box_zoom,reset,save,box_select"
+
+p1 = figure(title="Legend Example", tools=TOOLS)
+
+p1.scatter(x,   y, legend_label="sin(x)")
+p1.scatter(x, 2*y, legend_label="2*sin(x)", color="orange")
+p1.scatter(x, 3*y, legend_label="3*sin(x)", color="green")
+
+p1.legend.title = "Markers"
+
+p2 = figure(title="Another Legend Example", tools=TOOLS)
+
+p2.scatter(x, y, legend_label="sin(x)")
+p2.line(x, y, legend_label="sin(x)")
+
+p2.line(x, 2*y, legend_label="2*sin(x)",
+        line_dash=(4, 4), line_color="orange", line_width=2)
+
+p2.scatter(x, 3*y, legend_label="3*sin(x)",
+           marker="square", fill_color=None, line_color="green")
+p2.line(x, 3*y, legend_label="3*sin(x)", line_color="green")
+
+p2.legend.title = "Lines"
+
+toggle_legend = CustomAction(
+    icon=".bk-tool-icon-list",
+    description="Toggle legend",
+    callback=CustomJS(
+        args=dict(legends=p1.legend + p2.legend),
+        code="""
+        export default ({legends}) => {
+            for (const legend of legends) {
+                legend.visible = !legend.visible
+            }
+        }
+        """,
+    ),
+    active_callback=CustomJS(","path":"examples/interaction/tools/legend_visibility_tool.py","commit_id":"c92e5b2a5ae7c4932608195bd61f0302f76d90f7","original_commit_id":"255496b45411bc72003ad44d79567bb4a34fa1ba","user":{"login":"mattpap","id":27475,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI3NDc1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/27475?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap","html_url":"https://github.com/mattpap","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"I added `active_callback="auto"`, which effectively implies `tool.active = !tool.active`, and exposed `active` (and `disabled`) property in `CustomAction`.  This allows to set a boolean initial state and to change state on click. Setting `active_callback=None` (the default) means that the tool isn't stateful. I may still consider adding `active=None` and removing `"auto"`, but that may conflict with the underlying definition of `active` in `Tool` model. {"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/pulls/comments/1872415951","pull_request_review_id":2483384891,"id":1872415951,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOADqB3M5vmszP","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,60 @@
+import numpy as np
+
+from bokeh.layouts import gridplot
+from bokeh.models import CustomAction, CustomJS
+from bokeh.plotting import figure, show
+
+x = np.linspace(0, 4*np.pi, 100)
+y = np.sin(x)
+
+TOOLS = "pan,wheel_zoom,box_zoom,reset,save,box_select"
+
+p1 = figure(title="Legend Example", tools=TOOLS)
+
+p1.scatter(x,   y, legend_label="sin(x)")
+p1.scatter(x, 2*y, legend_label="2*sin(x)", color="orange")
+p1.scatter(x, 3*y, legend_label="3*sin(x)", color="green")
+
+p1.legend.title = "Markers"
+
+p2 = figure(title="Another Legend Example", tools=TOOLS)
+
+p2.scatter(x, y, legend_label="sin(x)")
+p2.line(x, y, legend_label="sin(x)")
+
+p2.line(x, 2*y, legend_label="2*sin(x)",
+        line_dash=(4, 4), line_color="orange", line_width=2)
+
+p2.scatter(x, 3*y, legend_label="3*sin(x)",
+           marker="square", fill_color=None, line_color="green")
+p2.line(x, 3*y, legend_label="3*sin(x)", line_color="green")
+
+p2.legend.title = "Lines"
+
+toggle_legend = CustomAction(
+    icon=".bk-tool-icon-list",
+    description="Toggle legend",
+    callback=CustomJS(
+        args=dict(legends=p1.legend + p2.legend),
+        code="""
+        export default ({legends}) => {
+            for (const legend of legends) {
+                legend.visible = !legend.visible
+            }
+        }
+        """,
+    ),
+    active_callback=CustomJS(","path":"examples/interaction/tools/legend_visibility_tool.py","commit_id":"cdb347dede14f00a504ffb34d086a99067d81f06","original_commit_id":"255496b45411bc72003ad44d79567bb4a34fa1ba","user":{"login":"mattpap","id":27475,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI3NDc1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/27475?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap","html_url":"https://github.com/mattpap","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"I ### Browser name and version

chrome, brave, firefox

### Jupyter notebook / Jupyter Lab version

_No response_

### Expected behavior

* create a dialog, set visible=False
* display a dialog via callback that sets visible=True
* hide a dialog via callback that sets visible=False
* display a dialog again via callback that sets visible=True

Dialog should be displayed.

### Observed behavior

Dialog is not displayed the second time around.

### Example code

```Python
from bokeh.models import Button, Dialog, CustomJS
from bokeh.plotting import show
from bokeh.layouts import row

open_button = Button(label="Open Dialog", width=200)

close_button = Button(label="Close", width=100)

dialog = Dialog(
    title="Example Dialog",
    content=close_button,
    visible=False
)

open_button.js_on_click(CustomJS(
    args=dict(dialog=dialog),
    code="""
    console.log('open button clicked');
    dialog.visible = true;
    """
))

close_button.js_on_click(CustomJS(
    args=dict(dialog=dialog),
    code="""
    console.log('close button clicked');
    dialog.visible = false;
    """
))

layout = row(open_button, dialog)
show(layout)
```

### Stack traceback or browser console output

Console log shows that callbacks are propagated correctly.

QuadMesh elements display fine gray or white lines between individual cells. From the docs:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/19e982c9-125b-4147-99aa-eb0c2ef89c5a)

While with matplotlib backend, this is the default:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1d90c7cb-d9d8-4145-a2d4-1cd962e811f5)

When you have small cells with respect to plot size, these separating lines can really mess with the apparent colours (I think this may be what is seen [here](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/72448687/python-using-hvplot-quadmesh-for-xarray-data-shows-blended-colors-in-a-plot)). Even in the general case, I'd like to be able to have my `hv.QuadMesh` yield the same visual output as my `hv.Image`, i.e. a continuous area instead of patches of colour detached from each other.

However, I have not found a way to do that. Clearly grid lines are not to blame. Then I tried setting `line_alpha` and `line_width` to 0, which from [this issue](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviews/issues/4016) I thought would work, to no avail. Maybe this can be done with a hook.

Interestingly I have the result I want by default using pure Bokeh (with the `figure.quad` glyph which is used by Holoviews) whether you create a multi-cell quad or aggregating several quads.

MRE:

```python
import numpy as np
import holoviews as hv

hv.extension('bokeh')

n = 8  # Number of bins in each direction
xs = np.logspace(1, 3, n)
ys = np.linspace(1, 10, n)
zs = np.arange((n-1)**2).reshape(n-1, n-1)
hv.QuadMesh((xs, ys, zs)).opts(line_width=0, line_alpha=0)
```

- [x] I may be interested in making a pull request to address this

```plaintext
holoviews 1.20.0
Bokeh 3.5.2
```
</details>

QuadMesh elements display fine gray or white lines between individual cells. From the docs:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/19e982c9-125b-4147-99aa-eb0c2ef89c5a)

While with matplotlib backend, this is the default:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1d90c7cb-d9d8-4145-a2d4-1cd962e811f5)

When you have small cells with respect to plot size, these separating lines can really mess with the apparent colours (I think this may be what is seen [here](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/72448687/python-using-hvplot-quadmesh-for-xarray-data-shows-blended-colors-in-a-plot)). Even in the general case, I'd like to be able to have my `hv.QuadMesh` yield the same visual output as my `hv.Image`, i.e. a continuous area instead of patches of colour detached from each other.

However, I have not found a way to do that. Clearly grid lines are not to blame. Then I tried setting `line_alpha` and `line_width` to 0, which from [this issue](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviews/issues/4016) I thought would work, to no avail. Maybe this can be done with a hook.

Interestingly I have the result I want by default using pure Bokeh (with the `figure.quad` glyph which is used by Holoviews) whether you create a multi-cell quad or aggregating several quads.

MRE:

```python
import numpy as np
import holoviews as hv

hv.extension('bokeh')

n = 8  # Number of bins in each direction
xs = np.logspace(1, 3, n)
ys = np.linspace(1, 10, n)
zs = np.arange((n-1)**2).reshape(n-1, n-1)
hv.QuadMesh((xs, ys, zs)).opts(line_width=0, line_alpha=0)
```

- [x] I may be interested in making a pull request to address this

    <summary>Software Version Info</summary>
```plaintext
holoviews 1.20.0
Bokeh 3.5.2
```
</details>

QuadMesh elements display fine gray or white lines between individual cells. From the docs:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/19e982c9-125b-4147-99aa-eb0c2ef89c5a)

While with matplotlib backend, this is the default:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1d90c7cb-d9d8-4145-a2d4-1cd962e811f5)

When you have small cells with respect to plot size, these separating lines can really mess with the apparent colours (I think this may be what is seen [here](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/72448687/python-using-hvplot-quadmesh-for-xarray-data-shows-blended-colors-in-a-plot)). Even in the general case, I'd like to be able to have my `hv.QuadMesh` yield the same visual output as my `hv.Image`, i.e. a continuous area instead of patches of colour detached from each other.

However, I have not found a way to do that. Clearly grid lines are not to blame. Then I tried setting `line_alpha` and `line_width` to 0, which from [this issue](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviews/issues/4016) I thought would work, to no avail. Maybe this can be done with a hook.

Interestingly I have the result I want by default using pure Bokeh (with the `figure.quad` glyph which is used by Holoviews) whether you create a multi-cell quad or aggregating several quads.

MRE:

```python
import numpy as np
import holoviews as hv

hv.extension('bokeh')

n = 8  # Number of bins in each direction
xs = np.logspace(1, 3, n)
ys = np.linspace(1, 10, n)
zs = np.arange((n-1)**2).reshape(n-1, n-1)
hv.QuadMesh((xs, ys, zs)).opts(line_width=0, line_alpha=0)
```

- [x] I may be interested in making a pull request to address this

### Expected behavior

The Div text should be shown in the exported SVG file and no random temporary files should be created!

### Observed behavior

Exporting Bokeh layout or gridplot to SVG does not show Div elements. Moreover, the code generates a new temporary `html` files (with a random name) in the working directory every time the code is executed even when I have specified an `output_file` name. If `output_file` name is specified, then the temporary file creation is skipped at the first execution. 

### Example code

```Python
import numpy as np

from bokeh.plotting import figure, output_file
from bokeh.models import Div
import bokeh.layouts as layouts
from bokeh.io import export_svg

N = 10000

x = np.random.normal(0, np.pi, N)
y = np.sin(x) + np.random.normal(0, 0.2, N)

TOOLS = "pan,wheel_zoom,box_zoom,reset,save,box_select"

p = figure(tools=TOOLS, output_backend="svg")
p.scatter(x, y, alpha=0.1, nonselection_alpha=0.001)

div = Div(text='Plotting random')

fig = layouts.column(div, p)

output_file('testSVG.html')

export_svg(fig, filename='testSVG.svg')
```

Expected behavior

I expect, that a HoverTool is able to work with a BoxAnnotations in the background.

### Observed behavior

When hovering over the line in the area of the BoxAnnotation, the cursor changes from cross ("precision select") to an arrow with four arrows ("move") and at the edges to a two-sided arrow ("resize"). The HoverTool does not work in this area.

### Example code

```Python
from bokeh.layouts import grid
from bokeh.models import BoxAnnotation, HoverTool
from bokeh.plotting import figure, output_notebook, show
output_notebook()

def draw_figure(x, y, draw_box_annotation:bool = True):
    p = figure(height=300, width=300)
    lr = p.line(x, y)
    if draw_box_annotation:
        p.renderers.extend(
             [BoxAnnotation(fill_color='gray', fill_alpha=0.5, left=2, right=4)]
        )
    p.add_tools(HoverTool(tooltips=[("value", "$y")], renderers=[lr], mode='vline'))
    return p

x = [1,2,3,4,5]
y = [1,2,3,4,5]

show(grid([[draw_figure(x, y), draw_figure(x, y, draw_box_annotation=False)]]))
```

Problem description

It is really hard to come up with a good legend position that works for all input data, while keeping the plot compact, so inevitably it will obscure the underlying data for some data inputs, or will take up an overly large amount of screen real estate.

However, it is frequently necessary to either hide or show items, or just to know what you are looking at.

Being able to hide/show the legend as needed would be very helpful towards making plots that work pretty well on disparate datasets without spending too much time on legend positioning.

### Feature description

Add a button on the toolbar that allows you to show/hide the legend.

### Potential alternatives

Putting the legend outside the plot area works, however it then takes up more real estate.  Developing complex heuristics for where the legend can go and cover the least amount of data, but this can be error prone and complicated.

Allowing the legend to be dragged around is another feature request that could be valuable, but this seems simpler.

### Additional information

### Expected behavior

* create a dialog, set visible=False
* display a dialog via callback that sets visible=True
* hide a dialog via callback that sets visible=False
* display a dialog again via callback that sets visible=True

Dialog should be displayed.

### Observed behavior

Dialog is not displayed the second time around.

### Example code

```Python
from bokeh.models import Button, Dialog, CustomJS
from bokeh.plotting import show
from bokeh.layouts import row

open_button = Button(label="Open Dialog", width=200)

close_button = Button(label="Close", width=100)

dialog = Dialog(
    title="Example Dialog",
    content=close_button,
    visible=False
)

open_button.js_on_click(CustomJS(
    args=dict(dialog=dialog),
    code="""
    console.log('open button clicked');
    dialog.visible = true;
    """
))

close_button.js_on_click(CustomJS(
    args=dict(dialog=dialog),
    code="""
    console.log('close button clicked');
    dialog.visible = false;
    """
))

layout = row(open_button, dialog)
show(layout)
```

### Stack traceback or browser console output

Console log shows that callbacks are propagated correctly.

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/pulls/comments/1887741066","pull_request_review_id":2507615679,"id":1887741066,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOADqB3M5whKSK","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,121 @@
+import {expect} from "assertions"
+import type {PlotActions} from "../../../interactive"
+import {actions, xy} from "../../../interactive"
+import {display} from "../../_util"
+
+import type {PlotView} from "@bokehjs/models/plots/plot"
+import {Plot, Range1d, LinearAxis, BoxAnnotation} from "@bokehjs/models"
+import {no_repeated} from "@bokehjs/core/util/iterator"
+
+describe("BoxAnnotation", () => {
+
+  async function mkplot(box: BoxAnnotation): Promise<PlotView> {
+    const plot = new Plot({
+      width: 400,
+      height: 400,
+      min_border: 0,
+      x_range: new Range1d({start: 0, end: 1}),
+      y_range: new Range1d({start: 0, end: 1}),
+    })
+    plot.add_layout(box)
+    plot.add_layout(new LinearAxis(), "above")
+    plot.add_layout(new LinearAxis(), "left")
+    const {view} = await display(plot)
+    return view
+  }
+
+  function get_cursor(plot_view: PlotView): string {
+    return getComputedStyle(plot_view.canvas_view.events_el).cursor
+  }
+
+  async function get_cursors(ac: PlotActions, events: Iterable<UIEvent>): Promise<string[]> {
+    const cursors = []
+    for await (const _ of ac._emit(events)) {
+      cursors.push(get_cursor(ac.target))
+    }
+    return cursors
+  }
+
+  describe("when movable without handles", () => {
+    function mkbox() {
+      return new BoxAnnotation({
+        left: 0,
+        top: 0,
+        right: 0.5,
+        bottom: 0.5,
+        editable: true,
+        movable: "both",
+      })
+    }
+
+    it("should set cursor to \"grab\" inside box", async () => {
+      const plot_view = await mkplot(mkbox())
+      const ac = actions(plot_view, {units: "data"})
+
+      // inside box
+      await ac.hover(xy(0.25, 0.25))
+      expect(get_cursor(plot_view)).to.be.equal("grab")
+
+      // outside box
+      await ac.hover(xy(0.75, 0.75))
+      expect(get_cursor(plot_view)).to.be.equal("default")
+    })
+
+    it("should set cursor to \"grabbing\" when panning", async () => {
+      const plot_view = await mkplot(mkbox())
+      const ac = actions(plot_view, {units: "data"})
+
+      // First, hover to put the cursor in the correct initial state ("grab")
+      await ac.hover(xy(0.25, 0.25))
+
+      // Next, pan
+      const events = ac._pan({type: "line", xy0: xy(0.25, 0.25), xy1: xy(0.75, 0.75), n: 5})
+      const cursors = await get_cursors(ac, events)
+
+      expect([...no_repeated(cursors)]).to.be
+        .equal([...no_repeated(["grab", "grabbing", "grab"])])
+    })
+  })
+
+  describe("when movable with handles", () => {
+    function mkbox() {
+      return new BoxAnnotation({
+        left: 0,
+        top: 0,
+        right: 0.5,
+        bottom: 0.5,
+        editable: true,
+        movable: "both",
+        use_handles: true,
+      })
+    }
+
+    it("should set cursor to \"move\" in the middle", async () => {
+      const plot_view = await mkplot(mkbox())
+      const ac = actions(plot_view, {units: "data"})
+
+      // inside box
+      await ac.hover(xy(0.25, 0.25))
+      expect(get_cursor(plot_view)).to.be.equal("move")
+
+      // outside box
+      await ac.hover(xy(0.75, 0.75))
+      expect(get_cursor(plot_view)).to.be.equal("default")
+    })
+
+    it("should set cursor to \"move\" when panning", async () => {
+      const plot_view = await mkplot(mkbox())
+      const ac = actions(plot_view, {units: "data"})
+
+      // First, hover to put the cursor in the correct initial state ("move")
+      await ac.hover(xy(0.25, 0.25))
+
+      // Next, pan
+      const events = ac._pan({type: "line", xy0: xy(0.25, 0.25), xy1: xy(0.75, 0.75), n: 5})
+      const cursors = await get_cursors(ac, events)
+
+      expect(cursors).to.be
+        .equal(["move", "move", "default", "move", "move", "move", "move"])","path":"bokehjs/test/unit/models/annotations/box_annotation.ts","commit_id":"175ec51264456695556c64808de814f70d53b50e","original_commit_id":"35e12f4dc5a46887cd8272c4c27e3a258c604be1","user":{"login":"mattpap","id":27475,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI3NDc1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/27475?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap","html_url":"https://github.com/mattpap","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"My current understanding is that `cursors` first captures last two hover cursors. Then `default` is due to how pan detection works, when the cursor manages to leave the handle. Finally panning is detected and other `move` cursors belong to pan gesture. In my experiments this test is reliable enough to leave as-is, but improvements will need be done in future, though I'm not exactly sure how to improve things at the - [ ] tests added / passed

### Changes, screenshots

Default cursor for moving the box selection is now the hand "grab" cursor instead of the "move" cursor.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3f7d874a-65c5-487b-8298-043285498a8c)

When dragging the box selection, the cursor changes from "grab" to "grabbing".

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f40ae4b-4e2b-478e-8bbf-68b18a920254)

Box handle cursors are unchanged.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2885294b-bfe9-4d66-8dd4-a66af43f9e9c)

However, box handle cursors now persist while the handle is being dragged.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e52c1d7f-98a2-4b26-83eb-ced805c18e83)

[x] issues: fixes #13027
- [ ] tests added / passed
- [ ] release document entry (if new feature or API change)

### Changes, screenshots

Default cursor for moving the box selection is now the hand "grab" cursor instead of the "move" cursor.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3f7d874a-65c5-487b-8298-043285498a8c)

When dragging the box selection, the cursor changes from "grab" to "grabbing".

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f40ae4b-4e2b-478e-8bbf-68b18a920254)

Box handle cursors are unchanged.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2885294b-bfe9-4d66-8dd4-a66af43f9e9c)

However, box handle cursors now persist while the handle is being dragged.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e52c1d7f-98a2-4b26-83eb-ced805c18e83)

[x] issues: fixes #13027
- [ ] tests added / passed
- [ ] release document entry (if new feature or API change)

### Changes, screenshots

Default cursor for moving the box selection is now the hand "grab" cursor instead of the "move" cursor.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3f7d874a-65c5-487b-8298-043285498a8c)

When dragging the box selection, the cursor changes from "grab" to "grabbing".

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f40ae4b-4e2b-478e-8bbf-68b18a920254)

Box handle cursors are unchanged.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2885294b-bfe9-4d66-8dd4-a66af43f9e9c)

However, box handle cursors now persist while the handle is being dragged.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e52c1d7f-98a2-4b26-83eb-ced805c18e83)

[x] issues: fixes #13027
- [ ] tests added / passed
- [ ] release document entry (if new feature or API change)

### Changes, screenshots

Default cursor for moving the box selection is now the hand "grab" cursor instead of the "move" cursor.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3f7d874a-65c5-487b-8298-043285498a8c)

When dragging the box selection, the cursor changes from "grab" to "grabbing".

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f40ae4b-4e2b-478e-8bbf-68b18a920254)

Box handle cursors are unchanged.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2885294b-bfe9-4d66-8dd4-a66af43f9e9c)

However, box handle cursors now persist while the handle is being dragged.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e52c1d7f-98a2-4b26-83eb-ced805c18e83)

Problem description

I was running through the docs and became confused on [this stage](https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/blob/branch-3.7/docs/bokeh/source/docs/first_steps/first_steps_2.rst#rendering-circles).

You tell us to "switch a line() to a circle()"... however, the code shows no mention of the `circle()` being used.  Instead, you use `scatter()`.  

### Feature description

Clean up this area so it makes a bit more sense.  Either show us how to use `circle()` or show us how to use `scatter()`.

### Potential alternatives

Leave it as is.  Maybe it doesn't need to be changed?

### Additional information

### Expected behavior

DataTable should render when using `autosize_mode="fit_viewport"` or `autosize_mode="fit_columns"`.

### Observed behavior

When setting `autosize_mode="fit_viewport"` or `autosize_mode="fit_columns"` in the example (https://docs.bokeh.org/en/latest/docs/user_guide/interaction/widgets.html#datatable) the table won't render due to a JS error:
**`Error rendering Bokeh items: ReferenceError: row is not defined`**


### Example code

```Python
from datetime import date
from random import randint

from bokeh.io import show, curdoc
from bokeh.models import ColumnDataSource, DataTable, DateFormatter, TableColumn

data = dict(
        dates=[date(2014, 3, i+1) for i in range(10)],
        downloads=[randint(0, 100) for i in range(10)],
    )
source = ColumnDataSource(data)

columns = [
        TableColumn(field="dates", title="Date", formatter=DateFormatter()),
        TableColumn(field="downloads", title="Downloads"),
    ]
data_table = DataTable(source=source, columns=columns, width=400, height=280, autosize_mode="fit_viewport")

doc = curdoc()
doc.add_root(data_table)
```

there, 

Using your exemple from http://bokeh.pydata.org/en/latest/docs/user_guide/styling.html#outside-the-plot-area (that work well ) i tried to make an update about legend. And it didn't work... The x position is not taken into account but the y position is. 
Here is a verry simplified  example:

```
import numpy as np
from bokeh.models import Legend
from bokeh.plotting import figure, show, output_file
from bokeh.models.widgets import Button
from bokeh.io import curdoc
from bokeh.layouts import column

x = np.linspace(0, 4*np.pi, 100)
y = np.sin(x)


p = figure(toolbar_location="above")

r0 = p.circle(x, y)
r1 = p.line(x, y)

r2 = p.line(x, 2*y, line_dash=[4, 4], line_color="orange", line_width=2)

r3 = p.square(x, 3*y, fill_color=None, line_color="green")
r4 = p.line(x, 3*y, line_color="green")

legend = Legend(items=[
    ("sin(x)"   , [r0, r1]),
    ("2*sin(x)" , [r2]),
    ("3*sin(x)" , [r3, r4]),
], location=(0, -30))

p.add_layout(legend, 'right')

def update_graph():
    for el in p.renderers:
                if isinstance(el, Legend):
                    p.renderers.remove(el)
    legend_new = Legend(items=[
            ("sin(x)"   , [r0, r1]),
            ("2*sin(x)" , [r2]),
            ("3*sin(x)" , [r3, r4]),
            ], location=(0, -30))
    
    p.add_layout(legend_new, 'right')


update = Button(label="update")
update.on_click(update_graph)

curdoc().add_root(column(p, update))

```

Before: 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/44472836/49949769-beb8eb80-fef6-11e8-8560-5c5dad747d67.png)

After: 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/44472836/49949792-ce383480-fef6-11e8-95bd-4c88239cc81d.png)

Versions:
bokeh==1.0.0
Python 3.7.0
OS: Windows

Thank you in advance and have a nice day. ### Expected behavior

On mobile, with `HoverTool` tooltip active, touching on any figure area should pop up the tooltip, regardless of zoom level (using mobile pinch-zoom). For example, on a mobile device, see:

https://www.snowpacktracker.com/btac/stormtracker

In previous Bokeh versions (v3.0.2) touching anywhere in the figure area would pop up the hover tool to see actual data values. The release of v3.6.1 enabled touch scrolling to work on mobile, but seems to have altered the tooltip behavior from previous versions.

### Observed behavior

The tooltip seems to work if you leave your finger on the mobile screen, without any zooming in. If you pinch-zoom in, it does not work at all. And if you just touch once, it does not stay active.

1. Devices with proximity pointer (pen/stylus). In this case hover is triggered just by the mere proximity of the pointer, thus it doesn't interact with any other gestures, i.e. tap, press and pan, though it can be also triggered by means explained in the second case.
2. Devices without proximity pointer (users have to touch the device's screen). Here hover works in all situations, but triggers differently than in previous bokeh versions (prior to the redesign of handling of UI gestures). Previously it was sufficient to tap on the plot to trigger hover, but now the user has to press and move the pointer. This allows to disambiguate hover from tap and pan gestures, though not from press gesture. Disambiguation from pan gesture is  particularly neat and actually not intentional in the original design. This triggering mechanism may not be what users expect and are accustomed to, thus I'm leaving this issue open as a discussion.

Also to note, hover is a single pointer gesture, thus is won't trigger when any multi pointer gesture is involved, like pinch zoom. This is a design decision and hover could potentially made to trigger during multi pointer gestures or even support multiple pointers, though that would require a significant redesign of all tools supporting hover.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/comments/2466698658/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

### Changes, screenshots

Default cursor for moving the box selection is now the hand "grab" cursor instead of the "move" cursor.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3f7d874a-65c5-487b-8298-043285498a8c)

When dragging the box selection, the cursor changes from "grab" to "grabbing".

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f40ae4b-4e2b-478e-8bbf-68b18a920254)

Box handle cursors are unchanged.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2885294b-bfe9-4d66-8dd4-a66af43f9e9c)

However, box handle cursors now persist while the handle is being dragged.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e52c1d7f-98a2-4b26-83eb-ced805c18e83)

#### Description of expected behavior and the observed behavior
Scalebar text removed when plot included in Layout

#### Complete, minimal, self-contained example code that reproduces the issue

```python
import holoviews as hv
hv.extension('bokeh')
curve = hv.Curve([1,2]).opts(scalebar=True)
curve
```

```python
curve + hv.Curve([2,1])
```

I think this and #6448 are problem in Bokeh (or a setting that we are not setting correctly).
> 
> A pure Bokeh example:
> ``` python
> import numpy as np
> from bokeh.layouts import column, row, gridplot
> from bokeh.models import ColumnDataSource, Metric, RangeTool, ScaleBar
> from bokeh.plotting import figure, show
> 
> n_points = 3000
> x_values = np.linspace(0, 100, n_points)
> y_values = np.random.randn(n_points).cumsum()
> 
> 
> def plot(width, height, location="right"):
>     source = ColumnDataSource(data=dict(x=x_values, y=y_values))
> 
>     p = figure(width=width, height=height)
>     p.line("x", "y", source=source)
>     p.toolbar_location = location
> 
>     scale_bar = ScaleBar(
>         range=p.x_range, orientation="horizontal", location="bottom_right"
>     )
>     p.add_layout(scale_bar)
>     return p
> 
> lay = row(plot(300, 300),  plot(800, 300))
> show(lay)
> 
> lay = gridplot(children=[[plot(300, 300, "above"), plot(300, 300, "above")]])
> show(lay)
> ```
> 
> ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dd89aa18-8795-45a0-9787-63f4de327756)
> 
> @mattpap, any thoughts? 

 _Originally posted by @hoxbro in [#6449](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviews/issues/6449#issuecomment-2470389879)_

I think this and #6448 are problem in Bokeh (or a setting that we are not setting correctly).
> 
> A pure Bokeh example:
> ``` python
> import numpy as np
> from bokeh.layouts import column, row, gridplot
> from bokeh.models import ColumnDataSource, Metric, RangeTool, ScaleBar
> from bokeh.plotting import figure, show
> 
> n_points = 3000
> x_values = np.linspace(0, 100, n_points)
> y_values = np.random.randn(n_points).cumsum()
> 
> 
> def plot(width, height, location="right"):
>     source = ColumnDataSource(data=dict(x=x_values, y=y_values))
> 
>     p = figure(width=width, height=height)
>     p.line("x", "y", source=source)
>     p.toolbar_location = location
> 
>     scale_bar = ScaleBar(
>         range=p.x_range, orientation="horizontal", location="bottom_right"
>     )
>     p.add_layout(scale_bar)
>     return p
> 
> lay = row(plot(300, 300),  plot(800, 300))
> show(lay)
> 
> lay = gridplot(children=[[plot(300, 300, "above"), plot(300, 300, "above")]])
> show(lay)
> ```
> 
> ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dd89aa18-8795-45a0-9787-63f4de327756)
> 
> @mattpap, any thoughts?

#### Description of expected behavior and the observed behavior
Scalebar text removed when plot included in Layout

#### Complete, minimal, self-contained example code that reproduces the issue

```python
import holoviews as hv
hv.extension('bokeh')
curve = hv.Curve([1,2]).opts(scalebar=True)
curve
```

```python
curve + hv.Curve([2,1])
```

### Expected behavior

On mobile, with `HoverTool` tooltip active, touching on any figure area should pop up the tooltip, regardless of zoom level (using mobile pinch-zoom). For example, on a mobile device, see:

https://www.snowpacktracker.com/btac/stormtracker

In previous Bokeh versions (v3.0.2) touching anywhere in the figure area would pop up the hover tool to see actual data values. The release of v3.6.1 enabled touch scrolling to work on mobile, but seems to have altered the tooltip behavior from previous versions.

### Observed behavior

The tooltip seems to work if you leave your finger on the mobile screen, without any zooming in. If you pinch-zoom in, it does not work at all. And if you just touch once, it does not stay active.

feature"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-11-14T10:58:25Z","updated_at":"2024-11-15T21:38:34Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"When first creating Panel I exposed all the base styling and layout related parameters from Bokeh, including `sizing_mode`, `width_policy` and `height_policy`. The thing is though that the width and height policy options effectively offers no more control than the `sizing_mode` and also less intuitive. Therefore I propose to remove these parameters, making it clear that `sizing_mode` is how to control these options. 

The impact should be minimal as we steered most users towards `sizing_mode` from the very beginning and with a longer deprecation period with warnings we can give folks time to switch over.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/7492/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/7492/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/comments/2479972234","html_url":"https://github.com/holoviz/panel/issues/7492#issuecomment-2479972234","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/7492","id":2479972234,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCLF6Q86T0V-K","user":{"login":"mattpap","id":27475,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI3NDc1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/27475?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap","html_url":"https://github.com/mattpap","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-15T21:38:34Z","updated_at":"2024-11-15T21:38:34Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"The point of having width and height polices instead of sizing modes is to:

1. Have fine and individual control over sizing in either dimensions.
2. Allow adding new policies without having to invent new sizing modes (e.g. `fit` policy regardless if it actually works).
3. Policies should have closer relationship with CSS. Sizing modes were invented primarily for plots.
4. Sizing modes can affect more than just the policy in either dimension (e.g. they can affect aspect management).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/comments/2479972234/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Software versions

Python version        :  3.12.4 (tags/v3.12.4:8e8a4ba, Jun  6 2024, 19:30:16) [MSC v.1940 64 bit (AMD64)]
IPython version       :  8.25.0
Tornado version       :  6.4.1
NumPy version         :  1.26.4
Bokeh version         :  3.6.1
BokehJS static path   :  ~/Envs/fbpinn/Lib/site-packages/bokeh/server/static)
node.js version       :  v20.17.0
npm version           :  10.8.2
jupyter_bokeh version :  4.0.5
Operating system      :  Windows-10-10.0.19045-SP0


### Expected behavior

I expect, that I can save a figure to a file as svg and when I open it another program, it is displayed correctly.

### Observed behavior

When I save a plot as a figure using the latest version ob bokeh `3.6.1` and open this file in `inkscape` or in python using `reportlab`, colored areas are defected. The color is not evaluated correctly and mostly shown in black.

Please notice, that this defect is not visible, if I open the file in a modern browser. Then the newer version is displayed in the correct from.

Reading the source code of the older and the newer versions shows, that the values inside an `rgb` call is changed. The older was `rgb(222,222,222)` which looks like valid CSS, while the newer is `rgb(222 222 222)` which looks like SCSS/SASS.

|3.4.3|3.6.1|
|--|--|
|![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/46984d30-b85d-4ffe-ada0-3b4a003d787c)|![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1daa763f-ca5d-4c7b-836e-4c4bcb04b995)|
|`<path fill="rgb(202,178,214)" stroke="rgb(202,178,214)">`|`<path fill="rgb(202 178 214)" stroke="rgb(202 178 214)">`|



### Example code

```Python
import bokeh
from bokeh.plotting import figure, show, output_notebook
output_notebook()

v = bokeh.__version__
p = figure(title=f"SVG export in {v}", tools="")
p.rect(x=[1, 2, 3], y=[1, 2, 3], width=1, height=1, color="#CAB2D6")
p.output_backend = 'svg'
from bokeh.io import export_svg

export_svg(p, filename=f"plot_{v}.svg")
show(p)
```

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/pulls/comments/1860392951","pull_request_review_id":2464592900,"id":1860392951,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOADqB3M5u41f3","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,121 @@
+import {expect} from "assertions"
+import type {PlotActions} from "../../../interactive"
+import {actions, xy} from "../../../interactive"
+import {display} from "../../_util"
+
+import type {PlotView} from "@bokehjs/models/plots/plot"
+import {Plot, Range1d, LinearAxis, BoxAnnotation} from "@bokehjs/models"
+import {no_repeated} from "@bokehjs/core/util/iterator"
+
+describe("BoxAnnotation", () => {
+
+  async function mkplot(box: BoxAnnotation): Promise<PlotView> {
+    const plot = new Plot({
+      width: 400,
+      height: 400,
+      min_border: 0,
+      x_range: new Range1d({start: 0, end: 1}),
+      y_range: new Range1d({start: 0, end: 1}),
+    })
+    plot.add_layout(box)
+    plot.add_layout(new LinearAxis(), "above")
+    plot.add_layout(new LinearAxis(), "left")
+    const {view} = await display(plot)
+    return view
+  }
+
+  function get_cursor(plot_view: PlotView): string {
+    return getComputedStyle(plot_view.canvas_view.events_el).cursor
+  }
+
+  async function get_cursors(ac: PlotActions, events: Iterable<UIEvent>): Promise<string[]> {
+    const cursors = []
+    for await (const _ of ac._emit(events)) {
+      cursors.push(get_cursor(ac.target))
+    }
+    return cursors
+  }
+
+  describe("when movable without handles", () => {
+    function mkbox() {
+      return new BoxAnnotation({
+        left: 0,
+        top: 0,
+        right: 0.5,
+        bottom: 0.5,
+        editable: true,
+        movable: "both",
+      })
+    }
+
+    it("should set cursor to \"grab\" inside box", async () => {
+      const plot_view = await mkplot(mkbox())
+      const ac = actions(plot_view, {units: "data"})
+
+      // inside box
+      await ac.hover(xy(0.25, 0.25))
+      expect(get_cursor(plot_view)).to.be.equal("grab")
+
+      // outside box
+      await ac.hover(xy(0.75, 0.75))
+      expect(get_cursor(plot_view)).to.be.equal("default")
+    })
+
+    it("should set cursor to \"grabbing\" when panning", async () => {
+      const plot_view = await mkplot(mkbox())
+      const ac = actions(plot_view, {units: "data"})
+
+      // First, hover to put the cursor in the correct initial state ("grab")
+      await ac.hover(xy(0.25, 0.25))
+
+      // Next, pan
+      const events = ac._pan({type: "line", xy0: xy(0.25, 0.25), xy1: xy(0.75, 0.75), n: 5})
+      const cursors = await get_cursors(ac, events)
+
+      expect([...no_repeated(cursors)]).to.be
+        .equal([...no_repeated(["grab", "grabbing", "grab"])])
+    })
+  })
+
+  describe("when movable with handles", () => {
+    function mkbox() {
+      return new BoxAnnotation({
+        left: 0,
+        top: 0,
+        right: 0.5,
+        bottom: 0.5,
+        editable: true,
+        movable: "both",
+        use_handles: true,
+      })
+    }
+
+    it("should set cursor to \"move\" in the middle", async () => {
+      const plot_view = await mkplot(mkbox())
+      const ac = actions(plot_view, {units: "data"})
+
+      // inside box
+      await ac.hover(xy(0.25, 0.25))
+      expect(get_cursor(plot_view)).to.be.equal("move")
+
+      // outside box
+      await ac.hover(xy(0.75, 0.75))
+      expect(get_cursor(plot_view)).to.be.equal("default")
+    })
+
+    it("should set cursor to \"move\" when panning", async () => {
+      const plot_view = await mkplot(mkbox())
+      const ac = actions(plot_view, {units: "data"})
+
+      // First, hover to put the cursor in the correct initial state ("move")
+      await ac.hover(xy(0.25, 0.25))
+
+      // Next, pan
+      const events = ac._pan({type: "line", xy0: xy(0.25, 0.25), xy1: xy(0.75, 0.75), n: 5})
+      const cursors = await get_cursors(ac, events)
+
+      expect(cursors).to.be
+        .equal(["move", "move", "default", "move", "move", "move", "move"])","path":"bokehjs/test/unit/models/annotations/box_annotation.ts","commit_id":"175ec51264456695556c64808de814f70d53b50e","original_commit_id":"35e12f4dc5a46887cd8272c4c27e3a258c604be1","user":{"login":"mattpap","id":27475,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI3NDc1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/27475?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap","html_url":"https://github.com/mattpap","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mattpap/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"The original test suffers similar behavior on occasion, so this isn't unexpected, especially given the overall fragility of cursor handling (specifically in `UIEvents`). ### Changes, screenshots

Default cursor for moving the box selection is now the hand "grab" cursor instead of the "move" cursor.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3f7d874a-65c5-487b-8298-043285498a8c)

When dragging the box selection, the cursor changes from "grab" to "grabbing".

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f40ae4b-4e2b-478e-8bbf-68b18a920254)

Box handle cursors are unchanged.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2885294b-bfe9-4d66-8dd4-a66af43f9e9c)

However, box handle cursors now persist while the handle is being dragged.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e52c1d7f-98a2-4b26-83eb-ced805c18e83)

[x] issues: fixes #13027
- [ ] tests added / passed
- [ ] release document entry (if new feature or API change)

### Changes, screenshots

Default cursor for moving the box selection is now the hand "grab" cursor instead of the "move" cursor.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3f7d874a-65c5-487b-8298-043285498a8c)

When dragging the box selection, the cursor changes from "grab" to "grabbing".

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f40ae4b-4e2b-478e-8bbf-68b18a920254)

Box handle cursors are unchanged.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2885294b-bfe9-4d66-8dd4-a66af43f9e9c)

However, box handle cursors now persist while the handle is being dragged.

![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e52c1d7f-98a2-4b26-83eb-ced805c18e83)

+""" Floating UI elements. """

+# Boilerplate

+from __future__ import annotations
+

+log = logging.getLogger(__name__)

+from ...core.enums import Location
+from ...core.properties import (

+    Required,

+from .panes import Pane

+#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

+
+__all__ = (
+    "Drawer",

+
+#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# General API
+#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+class Drawer(Pane):
+    """ A floating panel attachable to an edge of the viewport or a component.
+    """
+
+    # explicit __init__ to support Init signatures
+    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs) -> None:
+        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
+
+    location = Required(Enum(Location))(help="""

+    Otherwise add it to another UI component's ``elements`` property.
+    """)
+
+    open = Bool(default=False, help="""
+    Initial or actual state of the component.
+    """)
+
+    size = Either(Float, CSSLength)(default=300, help="""

```py
import numpy as np

from bokeh.models import Drawer, Examiner
from bokeh.plotting import figure, show

N = 4000
x = np.random.random(size=N) * 100
y = np.random.random(size=N) * 100
radii = np.random.random(size=N) * 1.5
colors = np.array([(r, g, 150) for r, g in zip(50+2*x, 30+2*y)], dtype="uint8")

plot = figure()
plot.circle(x, y, radius=radii, fill_color=colors, fill_alpha=0.6, line_color=None)

examiner = Examiner(target=plot)
drawer = Drawer(location="right", resizable=True, elements=[examiner])

show([plot, drawer])

<details>
<summary>Code</summary>

```py
import numpy as np

from bokeh.models import Drawer, Examiner
from bokeh.plotting import figure, show

N = 4000
x = np.random.random(size=N) * 100
y = np.random.random(size=N) * 100
radii = np.random.random(size=N) * 1.5
colors = np.array([(r, g, 150) for r, g in zip(50+2*x, 30+2*y)], dtype="uint8")

plot = figure()
plot.circle(x, y, radius=radii, fill_color=colors, fill_alpha=0.6, line_color=None)

examiner = Examiner(target=plot)
drawer = Drawer(location="right", resizable=True, elements=[examiner])

show([plot, drawer])
```

<details>
<summary>Code</summary>

```py
import numpy as np

from bokeh.models import Drawer, Examiner
from bokeh.plotting import figure, show

N = 4000
x = np.random.random(size=N) * 100
y = np.random.random(size=N) * 100
radii = np.random.random(size=N) * 1.5
colors = np.array([(r, g, 150) for r, g in zip(50+2*x, 30+2*y)], dtype="uint8")

plot = figure()
plot.circle(x, y, radius=radii, fill_color=colors, fill_alpha=0.6, line_color=None)

examiner = Examiner(target=plot)
drawer = Drawer(location="right", resizable=True, elements=[examiner])

show([plot, drawer])
```

PR enables auto mode for box zoom by default (previously `'both'`), see also https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/pull/12277
Additionally I have also enhanced the algorithm for switching between `'both'`, `'width'` and `'height'`:

- Increased pixel tolerance from 5 -> 15 to make it easier when selecting a large range
- Introduced an additional aspect ratio tolerance in order to avoid flickering: previously when drawing a box with close to an aspect ratio of 1 it was possible to temporarily switch from `'both'` to `'width' `or `'height'` and then back, e.g. in case of `dx=tol`, `dy=tol-1`

- [x] issues: fixes #14030

PR enables auto mode for box zoom by default (previously `'both'`), see also https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/pull/12277
Additionally I have also enhanced the algorithm for switching between `'both'`, `'width'` and `'height'`:

- Increased pixel tolerance from 5 -> 15 to make it easier when selecting a large range
- Introduced an additional aspect ratio tolerance in order to avoid flickering: previously when drawing a box with close to an aspect ratio of 1 it was possible to temporarily switch from `'both'` to `'width' `or `'height'` and then back, e.g. in case of `dx=tol`, `dy=tol-1`

- [x] issues: fixes #14030

a very good outcome. I'm glad you acted quickly. For me the takeaway is that we urgently need to set up some form of testing, even rudimentary unit testing (ideally) with code coverage, of Chromium's release candidates. As a secondary goal, we need to apply such base tests to other browsers and hopefully their RCs. Our current strategy is to have very finely detailed tests focusing on one platform, and one browser and version, primarily due to technical reasons (baseline testing) and performance, but it's high time to have an orthogonal approach for multiple versions, platforms and vendors. Another takeaway is that we should change the default bokehjs' CDN URL versioning strategy from specific versions to specific branches, while preserving version specific approach as an option for e.g. highly security minded folks. What I mean is that instead of this in generated HTML output: ```html ``` we should have this: ```html ``` which would resolve to the most recent patch version of 3.7. If we used that approach already and assuming that Chromium kept the offending change, then we would be able to publish new patch releases for all affected versions and no user intervention would be needed to take advantage of of these releases. Well, or something equivalent, e.g. learning from existing CDNs, like jsdelivr, which support npm's versioning strategies, like `~3.7` meaning the latest patch version in 3.7. In any case, to allow this, we will have to be stringent to have very focused and non-breaking patch releases, which in fact we managed to have for a while now.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/comments/2413504947/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

(...) I have no idea whether it's actually covered by a standard somewhere, or if we've just gotten "lucky" with a common result for undefined behavior all these years. I originally thought this was some form of UB, but upon reading [Web IDL](https://webidl.spec.whatwg.org/) standard specification, which is used to define concrete Web APIs like HTML Canvas API, I think that the original and restored behavior is the correct and well defined one. Here's the excerpt of relevant definitions from [HTML Canvas](https://html.spec.whatwg.org/#2dcontext) specification: ```ts interface mixin CanvasDrawPath { undefined fill(optional CanvasFillRule fillRule = "nonzero"); undefined fill(Path2D path, optional CanvasFillRule fillRule = "nonzero"); } ``` Let's focus on the first one, but the reasoning is equivalent in both cases. First of all, in section [2.5.8.1 Overloading vs. union types](https://webidl.spec.whatwg.org/#idl-overloading-vs-union): > Specification authors are encouraged to treat missing argument and `undefined` argument the same way in the JavaScript language binding. and: > (...) specification authors are encouraged to use an `optional` argument, which would categorize both `foo()` and `foo(undefined)` as "arg is missing". Then in section [3.6 Overload resolution algorithm](https://webidl.spec.whatwg.org/#js-overloads), step 11.4.1 we get: > If optionality is “optional” and V is undefined, then: > If the argument at index i is declared with a default value, then append to values that default value. and a note regarding the algorithm: > When converting an optional argument’s JavaScript value to its equivalent IDL value, `undefined` will be converted into the optional argument’s default value, if it has one, or a special value “missing” otherwise. Altogether we get that `ctx.fill()` and `ctx.fill(undefined)` are equivalent, and imply `ctx.fill("nonzero")`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/comments/2414730281/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

PR enables auto mode for box zoom by default (previously `'both'`), see also https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/pull/12277
Additionally I have also enhanced the algorithm for switching between `'both'`, `'width'` and `'height'`:

- Increased pixel tolerance from 5 -> 15 to make it easier when selecting a large range
- Introduced an additional aspect ratio tolerance in order to avoid flickering: previously when drawing a box with close to an aspect ratio of 1 it was possible to temporarily switch from `'both'` to `'width' `or `'height'` and then back, e.g. in case of `dx=tol`, `dy=tol-1`

- [x] issues: fixes #14030

PR enables auto mode for box zoom by default (previously `'both'`), see also https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/pull/12277
Additionally I have also enhanced the algorithm for switching between `'both'`, `'width'` and `'height'`:

- Increased pixel tolerance from 5 -> 15 to make it easier when selecting a large range
- Introduced an additional aspect ratio tolerance in order to avoid flickering: previously when drawing a box with close to an aspect ratio of 1 it was possible to temporarily switch from `'both'` to `'width' `or `'height'` and then back, e.g. in case of `dx=tol`, `dy=tol-1`

- [x] issues: fixes #14030

default","user":{"login":"muendlein","id":71090451,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjcxMDkwNDUx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/71090451?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein","html_url":"https://github.com/muendlein","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This PR enables auto mode for box zoom by default (previously `'both'`), see also https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/pull/12277
Additionally I have also enhanced the algorithm for switching between `'both'`, `'width'` and `'height'`:

- Increased pixel tolerance from 5 -> 15 to make it easier when selecting a large range
- Introduced an additional aspect ratio tolerance in order to avoid flickering: previously when drawing a box with close to an aspect ratio of 1 it was possible to temporarily switch from `'both'` to `'width' `or `'height'` and then back, e.g. in case of `dx=tol`, `dy=tol-1`

- [x] issues: fixes #14030

{"action":"closed","number":14091,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/pulls/14091","id":2106517723,"node_id":"PR_kwDOADqB3M59jujb","html_url":"https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/pull/14091","diff_url":"https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/pull/14091.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/pull/14091.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/14091","number":14091,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"enable auto box zoom by default","user":{"login":"muendlein","id":71090451,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjcxMDkwNDUx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/71090451?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein","html_url":"https://github.com/muendlein","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/muendlein/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This PR enables auto mode for box zoom by default (previously `'both'`), see also https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/pull/12277
Additionally I have also enhanced the algorithm for switching between `'both'`, `'width'` and `'height'`:

- Increased pixel tolerance from 5 -> 15 to make it easier when selecting a large range
- Introduced an additional aspect ratio tolerance in order to avoid flickering: previously when drawing a box with close to an aspect ratio of 1 it was possible to temporarily switch from `'both'` to `'width' `or `'height'` and then back, e.g. in case of `dx=tol`, `dy=tol-1`

- [x] issues: fixes #14030

Problem description

Currently the box zoom tool can be configured to handle 2D and 1D zooming. However this be behavior must be set up front and is not supported at run time.

### Feature description

2D zooming (rectangle) should turn into 1D zooming at runtime if the rectangle reaches a very high aspect ratio (close to a vertical or horizontal line). This is especially useful to switch between zooming in x and y directions.
Note: This behavior is implemented in many other popular plotting framework and thus should be considered to be the default.

This workflow is essentially equivalent to bokehjs' default workflow, but instead of running image baseline tests, this workflow only generates images, but doesn't compare them, because they would fail most of the time anyway. Note that this workflow may fail regardless, so inspection of the output is needed every time.

There is one optional argument to the workflow, which allows to specify the snap channel to install Chromium from. The default is `beta`. To determine what version is associated with each channel, use:
```sh
$ snap info chromium
(...)
channels:
  latest/stable:    129.0.6668.100 2024-10-11 (2972) 181MB -
  latest/candidate: 130.0.6723.44  2024-10-17 (2977) 180MB -
  latest/beta:      130.0.6723.44  2024-10-14 (2973) 181MB -
  latest/edge:      131.0.6753.0   2024-10-08 (2970) 184MB -
```","created_at":"2024-10-18T06:32:43Z","updated_at":"2024-10-18T06:32:43Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":null,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[{"id":83307828,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw4MzMwNzgyOA==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/labels/tag:%20component:%20tests","name":"tag: ### Expected behavior

Mobile touch scrolling should be functional for bokeh applications (within any bokeh figure).

### Observed behavior

Mobile touch scrolling is non-functional after updating from Bokeh 3.0.2 to 3.6.0.

Here is my web application, where touch scrolling is non-functional after updating versions:

https://www.snowpacktracker.com/btac/stormtracker

I assume this could be true for many bokeh applications, when viewed on a mobile device? I am not sure if this is a recently introduced Bokeh bug, or something to do with how I am serving my application. However, my only change was upgrading Bokeh version.

### Example code

I can try to provide a minimal/reproducible example, but it is somewhat complex to completely reproduce my production Flask app. To start, in Bokeh my `figure` instances are all put into a `layout` such as:

```
doc = curdoc()

hor_layout_select = (
        row(
          column(hor_layout_date_slider),
          column(time_select_widget),
          column(zone_select_widget),
          column(help_spacer,help_button)
        )
    )

children = [hor_layout_select]

for f in figs:
    children.append(f)

l = layout(children, sizing_mode='stretch_width')

doc.add_root(l)
```

I am building my app as a Flask application, where I embed a Bokeh server document such as:

`bokeh.embed.server_document(bokeh_url, resources=None)`

I then use Flask's `render_template()` passing the embedded server doc and a `base.html` (which fetches bokeh js, widgets and tables resources via CDN). I am using some minimal custom css to style the container that holds the bokeh application, but I am not enabling any custom scrolling or mobile functionality in any html or css.

### Expected behavior

Mobile touch scrolling should be functional for bokeh applications (within any bokeh figure).

### Observed behavior

Mobile touch scrolling is non-functional after updating from Bokeh 3.0.2 to 3.6.0.

Here is my web application, where touch scrolling is non-functional after updating versions:

https://www.snowpacktracker.com/btac/stormtracker

I assume this could be true for many bokeh applications, when viewed on a mobile device? I am not sure if this is a recently introduced Bokeh bug, or something to do with how I am serving my application. However, my only change was upgrading Bokeh version.

### Example code

I can try to provide a minimal/reproducible example, but it is somewhat complex to completely reproduce my production Flask app. To start, in Bokeh my `figure` instances are all put into a `layout` such as:

```
doc = curdoc()

hor_layout_select = (
        row(
          column(hor_layout_date_slider),
          column(time_select_widget),
          column(zone_select_widget),
          column(help_spacer,help_button)
        )
    )

children = [hor_layout_select]

for f in figs:
    children.append(f)

l = layout(children, sizing_mode='stretch_width')

doc.add_root(l)
```

I am building my app as a Flask application, where I embed a Bokeh server document such as:

`bokeh.embed.server_document(bokeh_url, resources=None)`

I then use Flask's `render_template()` passing the embedded server doc and a `base.html` (which fetches bokeh js, widgets and tables resources via CDN). I am using some minimal custom css to style the container that holds the bokeh application, but I am not enabling any custom scrolling or mobile functionality in any html or css.

adds a separate on demand bokehjs workflow for testing new versions of Chromium (issue #14090). This workflow is essentially equivalent to bokehjs' default workflow, but instead of running image baseline tests, this workflow only generates images, but doesn't compare them, because they would fail most of the time anyway. Note that this workflow may fail regardless, so inspection of the output is needed every time.

There is one optional argument to the workflow, which allows to specify the snap channel to install Chromium from. The default is `beta`. To determine what version is associated with each channel, use:
```sh
$ snap info chromium
(...)
channels:
  latest/stable:    129.0.6668.100 2024-10-11 (2972) 181MB -
  latest/candidate: 130.0.6723.44  2024-10-17 (2977) 180MB -
  latest/beta:      130.0.6723.44  2024-10-14 (2973) 181MB -
  latest/edge:      131.0.6753.0   2024-10-08 (2970) 184MB -

This workflow is essentially equivalent to bokehjs' default workflow, but instead of running image baseline tests, this workflow only generates images, but doesn't compare them, because they would fail most of the time anyway. Note that this workflow may fail regardless, so inspection of the output is needed every time.

There is one optional argument to the workflow, which allows to specify the snap channel to install Chromium from. The default is `beta`. To determine what version is associated with each channel, use:
```sh
$ snap info chromium
(...)
channels:
  latest/stable:    129.0.6668.100 2024-10-11 (2972) 181MB -
  latest/candidate: 130.0.6723.44  2024-10-17 (2977) 180MB -
  latest/beta:      130.0.6723.44  2024-10-14 (2973) 181MB -
  latest/edge:      131.0.6753.0   2024-10-08 (2970) 184MB -
```","created_at":"2024-10-18T06:32:43Z","updated_at":"2024-10-21T19:23:38Z","closed_at":"2024-10-21T19:23:37Z","merged_at":"2024-10-21T19:23:37Z","merge_commit_sha":"51531c05fabaf6adf81cf4b046b3e11204b7feeb","assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[{"id":83307828,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw4MzMwNzgyOA==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/labels/tag:%20component:%20tests","name":"tag:
